name: White Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1/4 C. Cointreau or other orange-flavored liqueur
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine graham cracker crumbs, ground pecans, and melted butter or margarine in a bowl. Stir until blended.
2. Press the mixture into the bottom and 1 inch up the sides of a 9-inch spring-form pan.
3. Bake at 375 degrees F for 8 minutes; cool.
4. Place white chocolate in the top of a double boiler over simmering water. Reduce heat to low and cook until melted, stirring occasionally.
5. Let cool slightly. Combine cream cheese and sour cream in a large mixing bowl; beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until fluffy.
6. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition. Stir in vanilla and chocolate until just blended.
7. Pour the filling into the prepared pan. Bake at 300 degrees F for 50 minutes or until set.
8. Turn off the oven; open the door partially. Leave the cake in the oven for 30 minutes. Cool on a wire rack; chill.
9. Drain mandarin oranges and reserve the liquid. Combine the oranges, Cointreau, orange juice, and cornstarch in a saucepan. Stir until thickened over medium heat.
10. Remove the outer rim of the spring-form pan. Place the cheesecake on a serving plate. Arrange orange sections in the center and around the edge.
11. Spoon glaze over the top of the cheesecake; chill.
Yield: one 9-inch cheesecake.
